hallucination.rationale: The note contains numerous fabricated findings with no basis in the transcript: vital signs (BP 130/80, HR 100, Temp 36.2, RR 20, SpO2 98%), shortness of breath and cough, nausea and vomiting, joint pain and muscle weakness, lab work orders, X-ray, MRI, ultrasound, kidney stones. The transcript explicitly states no imaging is indicated, yet the note orders MRI, X-ray, and ultrasound.
flagged_content: Mr. Okonkwo has shortness of breath and cough
Mr. Okonkwo has nausea and vomiting
Mr. Okonkwo has joint pain and muscle weakness
Mr. Okonkwo will have blood work done to check for any abnormalities
He will also have an X-ray to check for any fractures or other injuries
Mr. Okonkwo will have an MRI to check for any injuries to the spine or surrounding tissues
He will also have an ultrasound to check for any kidney stones or other issues
Mr. Okonkwo has normal vision, hearing, and normal respiratory sounds. He has no difficulty walking or speaking
no recent history of yard work

instruction_following.rationale: The template instructs to only include systems that were discussed, yet the note includes fabricated ROS entries for HEENT, Cardiovascular, Respiratory, GI, Psychiatric, and Skin which were never discussed. The template says to only include vitals that were documented, yet fabricated vitals were included. The note fabricated diagnostic workup directly contradicting the transcript.

completeness.rationale: The note misses numerous key findings: the specific mechanism details (4 days ago), pain severity ratings (5-6/10 at rest, 8/10 with transitions), ibuprofen dosing was confused (stated twice daily instead of three times daily), lumbar flexion limited to 30 degrees, extension better tolerated, tenderness at L4-L5, paraspinal muscle spasm, negative SLR details, the assessment of acute mechanical low back strain, no imaging indicated, counseling about GI side effects, counseling about staying active/avoiding bed rest, ice/heat recommendations, physical therapy referral, expected recovery timeline, and ER return precautions.
missed_items: Pain severity 5-6/10 at rest, 8 with transitions
Onset 4 days ago on Saturday
Ibuprofen 400mg three times daily (not twice daily)
Lumbar flexion limited to 30 degrees
Extension better tolerated than flexion
Tenderness at L4-L5 level
Right paraspinal muscle tightness and spasm
Negative straight leg raise bilaterally with hamstring tightness
Patellar and Achilles reflexes normal and symmetric
Assessment of acute mechanical low back strain
No imaging indicated
Ibuprofen increased to 600mg TID with food for 7-10 days
GI side effect counseling
Cyclobenzaprine drowsiness counseling and driving restrictions
Stay active, avoid prolonged bed rest
Avoid heavy lifting for two weeks
Ice 20 minutes alternating with heat
Physical therapy referral for core strengthening
Recovery timeline 2-4 weeks significant improvement, 6 weeks baseline
Return precautions for numbness, tingling, weakness, bladder/bowel changes

template_adherence.rationale: While the note follows some template sections, the Assessment section is missing (merged into Plan), Past Medical History and Family History sections are missing, Vital Signs are placed after the general exam instead of before, and the Chief Complaint section contains HPI content rather than a concise complaint.

entity_marking.rationale: No entity marking was attempted anywhere in the note. No {{drug:...}} or {{condition:...}} syntax was used.

duplication.rationale: Multiple sections contain duplicated content: ibuprofen dosing is repeated multiple times in HPI and Medications, 'occasional mild back soreness with yard work' is stated twice in Constitutional, and the physical exam description is repeated in General.
